title: cornus hongkongensis subsp. tonkinensis
intro: Cornus hongkongensis subsp. tonkinensis, also known as the Tonkin Dogwood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that is native to southern China and northern Vietnam, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antibacterial activities. The Tonkin Dogwood is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation, lower body temperature, and prevent the growth of bacteria. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to promote overall health and well-being.
